title: Non-member of Federal Parliament to be Minister

content.text: of Article 76, the President may, on recommendation of the Prime Minister, appoint a person who is not a member of the Federal Parliament as a Minister
must obtain membership of the Federal Parliament within six months from the date of taking oath by him or her.
In the event of failure to obtain membership of the Federal Parliament within the period mentioned in clause
, he or she shall not be qualified to be reappointed to the office of Minister during the term of the then House of Representatives.

, a person who has been defeated in the election to the then House of Representatives shall not be qualified to be appointed to the office of Minister during the term of such House of Representatives.
